_note: Northwind (weak brand strategy) — the strategy sub-council's D1–D6 + bullshit filter.
_provenance: RE-SOURCED from real council transcripts (I-13 deeper fix): phrasings extracted from runs/2026-06-04-northwind-baseline.md, runs/2026-06-10-northwind-run2.md, runs/2026-06-10-northwind-run3.md — lightly trimmed (bylines/scaffolding removed), the core council wording kept verbatim. A green check-recall now means 'the checker's patterns still catch wordings the council really produced' (a measurement), not 'the patterns match the author's paraphrase of its own patterns'. NOTE: for D2 the council's pure verbatim quotes ('every premium coffee brand could publish this verbatim', 'could run under a … letterhead') originally did NOT match the checker patterns; check.py:D2 was WIDENED centrally (I-13) to catch them ('letterhead', 'publish … verbatim', '(any|every) … brand could'), so those bare council wordings now resolve directly and are included above — the brittle-pattern signal is closed, not worked around.

D1 borrowed cultural root (no real prove: the root is a competitor survey, not a world — the textbook landscape
a landscape survey wearing the heading 'Cultural roots' — borrowed cool, fully laundered; the receipts I asked for do not exist
every reference here is itself a brand — a moodboard of other people's conclusions; matching what's out there is the definition of having no roots
borrowed cultural root: an Apple/Aesop/Patagonia moodboard, not primary-source archaeology
D2 category-restatement position (not ex: D2 category restatement — a position any premium coffee brand could sign
a category restatement any premium coffee brand could sign
no zag — every premium coffee brand could publish this verbatim, so it restates the category instead of staking a claim
it doesn't merely lack a difference — it declares parity; a category restatement, not an ownable position
every premium coffee brand could publish this verbatim
it could run under a Blue Bottle or Nespresso letterhead and nothing would feel off
D4 no enemy / no tension: a brand that excludes no one is chosen by no one — no enemy, no tension
premium for everyone is a perceptual impossibility
we don't believe in excluding anyone quietly dismantles the whole premise — it stands for nothing
D5 persona instead of transformation: still Sarah, now holding a latte — a person, frozen, not a transformation
who does she get to become? before-self equals after-self; the brand changes nothing
a demographic persona, not a becoming — a snapshot, not a transformation
it captures who the customer is, not who they become
D6 values without trade-offs: tautologies you cannot disagree with — values without trade-offs
not one falsifiable sentence in the document — tautology in bullet form
a strategy you can't disagree with isn't a strategy — values that sacrifice nothing
ask what the brand would give up — nothing; these values cost nothing
bullshit filter: archetype / VMV doing s: the Explorer archetype is a horoscope doing a strategy's job
'The Explorer' cannot tell anyone what to make or what to refuse — a frozen label presented as a system
the archetype is a horoscope, not an engine
brand-type dishonesty across the vision and mission lines — boilerplate doing strategy's job
